payload.claim_text: The November Plan remains balanced through 2026 with deficits of $378 million in 2027 and $419 million in 2028, compared with the July Plan which was also balanced through 2026 and included deficits of $428 million in 2027 and $469 million in 2028.
payload.subject: 2024 MTA November Financial Plan

payload.claim_text: The February Plan baseline included operating efficiencies initiatives that are expected to generate $1.88 billion through 2027 directly impacting MTA. The November Plan includes an additional $312 million in savings, which have been incorporated into Agency baseline financial plans and meet the MTA annual $500 million savings target from Operating Efficiencies as of 2025.
payload.subject: MTA Operating Efficiencies

payload.claim_text: The Plan continues to reflect additional farebox and toll revenue from biennial 4 percent yield increases, which are proposed for August 2025 and March 2027.
payload.subject: MTA Financial Plan

payload.claim_text: The Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) tolls will be implemented in three steps. Phase 1 (2025-2027) equals 60% of March 2024 adopted toll structure values. Phase 2 (2028-2030) equals 80%. Full effect in 2031. TBTA Board adopted Phase-In Approach on November 18, 2024. TBTA began collecting CBDTP tolls on January 5, 2025.
payload.subject: Central Business District Tolling Program

payload.scope: combined 2020-2024 Capital Programs and 2020-2024 MTA Bridges and Tunnels Capital Program
payload.raw_value_text: $29,582 had been committed
Capital programs totaling $54,799 covering the years 2020-2024

payload.claim_text: The combined funding sources for the 2020–2024 MTA Capital Programs and the 2020-2024 MTA Bridges and Tunnels Capital Program, include $15,000 in Central Business District tolling sources, $10,000 in new revenue sources, $7,393 in MTA bonds and PAYGO, $3,327 in MTA Bridges and Tunnels bonds, $13,087 in Federal funds, $3,169 in State of New York funding, $3,007 in City of New York funding, and $589 in other contributions.
payload.subject: 2020-2024 Capital Program

payload.raw_value_text: the metropolitan area index increasing 4.2%
the national index increased 2.7%
New York Harbor spot price for conventional gasoline decreased by 10.6% from an average price of $2.35 per gallon to an average price of $2.10 per gallon between the fourth quarters of 2023 and 2024
Seasonally adjusted non-agricultural employment in New York City for the fourth quarter was higher in 2024 than in 2023 by 86.7 thousand jobs (1.8%)
the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) maintained its target for the Federal Funds rate at the 4.25% to 4.5% range

payload.event_name: MTA Board approval of 2020-2024 Capital Program
payload.description: The 2020-2024 Capital Programs totaling $54,799 were originally approved by the MTA Board

payload.claim_text: Additional risks to the November Plan include: (1) Continued paid ridership recovery; progress in reducing fare evasion is critical. (2) Paratransit reimbursement - assumes extension of legislation requiring NYC to fund 80% of net paratransit operating expenses. (3) MTA operating efficiencies need to be fully implemented and sustained. (4) Dedicated tax receipts could be impacted by economic slowdown. (5) Casino license and gaming tax revenues uncertain. (6) Approval and funding for 2025-2029 Capital Program needed; over-reliance on MTA debt could increase debt service costs.
payload.subject: MTA Financial Plan risks
